title: Identify the general form
text: The problem describes damped harmonic motion. Recall the general form for such a function, typically $$y=ae**{-ct}\cos(\omega $$t)$$ or $$y=ae**{-ct}\sin(\omega $$t)$$. $$Since$$ $$the$$ $$spring$$ $$is$$ $$compressed$$ $$and$$ $$released$$, $$it$$ $$starts$$ $$at$$ $$maximum$$ $$displacement$$, $$suggesting$$ $$a$$ $$cosine$$ $$function$$.

title: What is the initial amplitude?
text: What is the initial displacement (amplitude) A when the spring is released?

title: Determine angular frequency
text: The problem states the oscillation period. Use this to find the angular frequency $$\omega$$.

title: Calculate omega
text: Given that it oscillates once every $$3$$ seconds (period $$p=3\$\$)$$, $$what$$ $$is$$ $$\omega$$?

title: Determine the damping constant
text: The amplitude decreases by 30% every second. This information helps determine the damping constant $$c$$ in the $$e**{-ct}$$ term.
